title: 5 takeaways from the 4-1 U.S. World Cup win over Paraguay
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: World Cup The United States rode arguably the best World Cup half in the history of the men's team to an easy win.
Folarin Balogun, center, celebrates scoring for the United States in its World Cup opener against Paraguay.
AP Photo/Andre Penner By Hayden Bird June 13, 2026 | 12:03 AM 4 minutes to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Red Sox hit 3 home runs, explode for double-digit runs in win vs. Rangers
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Red Sox Boston played an all-around dominant game to win Friday night’s series opener.
Willson Contreras and Wilyer Abreu combined for six hits.
AP Photo/Charles Krupa ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Red Sox finally get good injury news as Willson Contreras dodges possible hamstring issue
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Boston Red Sox Contreras appeared to hurt his hamstring in the middle of Friday's game, but stayed in after a meeting with the training staff.
Willson Contreras did not suffer an injury on Friday despite a quick check in with Boston's training staff.
(AP Photo/Charles Krupa) The Red Sox seem to have finally caught a break from the injury bug Friday night.

title: Red Sox’ Caleb Durbin working with Yankees star Aaron Judge’s personal hitting coach
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Red Sox Durbin has consulted Richard Schenck dating back to 2025.
Caleb Durbin has sought external hitting help since joining the Red Sox this year.
AP Photo/Chris O'Meara ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Watch: David Ortiz’s son, D’Angelo, hits first Red Sox home run
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Red Sox The 21-year-old went deep for the first time since being drafted by Boston in 2024.
David Ortiz tips his cap to the crowd before playing the New York Yankees on Sept.
29, 2016, with D'Angelo by his side.

title: Garrett Crochet clarifies recent injury comments, but is still expected to miss significant time
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Boston Red Sox Even if Crochet slightly misspoke when addressing his injury, he's still expected to be out until after the All-Star break.
Garrett Crochet clarified his injury status Friday.

(AP Photo/Carolyn Kaster) Garrett Crochet’s injury return timeline remains a mystery to everyone, including seemingly the Red Sox themselves, who didn’t seem to be on the same page this week when discussing his ailments.

title: NFL closes its review of the Stefon Diggs allegations, saying there’s not enough evidence to punish him
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: New England Patriots The decision comes after Diggs was found not guilty of assault and strangulation charges last month.
Stefon Diggs AP Photo/Julio Cortez The NFL has decided that there’s not enough evidence to prove that Stefon Diggs violated its personal conduct policy.
The league’s review of the situation is now closed, according to multiple reports.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca and Emily Turner
bullets: Quizzes A Drake Maye collision, a new high-end Asian grocery store, and a walking trail are all part of this week’s Greater Boston news quiz.
New England Patriots quarterback Drake Maye (10) on the last day of Patriots minicamp on June 11, 2026.
